recentItems.title: Public Health Alerts: Andes hantavirus on a cruise ship, 2026 - CIDRAP
recentItems.summary: The 2026 MV Hondius outbreak of Andes hantavirus resulted in 13 confirmed cases and 4 deaths among passengers and crew, leading to the quarantine of 87 individuals aboard the ship; the World Health Organization stated this was the first documented instance of human-to-human transmission of the virus on a cruise vessel.

recentItems.title: Where Passengers From the Hantavirus-Struck Cruise Ship Are Now - The New York Times
recentItems.summary: A hantavirus outbreak on the 2026 MV Hondius cruise ship has resulted in one confirmed death and at least three other cases among passengers. All 2,500 passengers have been quarantined on board or at designated facilities, while the cruise line stated it is cooperating fully with health authorities.

recentItems.title: Hantavirus outbreak: WHO calls for active monitoring of all aboard MV Hondius after deaths - MSN
recentItems.summary: On the MV Hondius cruise ship, a hantavirus outbreak has resulted in several deaths (10 cases and 3 deaths as of the latest reports), prompting the WHO to call for active monitoring of all passengers and crew, though no official quarantine number has been specified; the WHO emphasized that "active monitoring is essential to contain the spread."

recentItems.summary: An outbreak of hantavirus aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship in 2026 has resulted in 12 confirmed cases and 3 deaths, with 85 passengers and crew placed under quarantine as of the latest reports.

recentItems.title: These are the U.S. states monitoring for hantavirus cases linked to cruise ship at center of outbreak - Yahoo
recentItems.summary: As of early 2026, 8 U.S. states—including Florida, Texas, and New York—are monitoring passengers from the MV Hondius cruise ship for hantavirus infections, following an outbreak with 27 confirmed cases and 6 deaths. Health officials have placed 312 passengers and crew under quarantine, with the CDC stating they are "working closely with state partners to track all potential exposures."
